assertions.text: Vehicle exited the curve before the collision (the slide occurred coming out of the curve).
Vehicle slid off the road prior to contacting the utility pole.
Vehicle contacted the utility pole (vehicle struck the pole).
The contact with the utility pole was a light, glancing contact.
There was a single impact (one contact) with the utility pole.
The vehicle required only cosmetic repairs after the contact.

verdicts.evidence: The assertion about exiting a curve before collision cannot be determined from the available sensor channels. Steering shows a peak signed deviation of [27.0](T2) degrees at [t=-0.2 s](T2) and baseline median [4.5](T2) deg, but there is no map/road-curve metadata to confirm 'coming out of the curve', so sequencing relative to a curve is unverifiable.

Whether the vehicle 'slid off the road' prior to contacting the pole is not directly recorded by the vehicle sensors provided. Speed changed from [83.0 km/h at t=-4.7 s to 80.0 km/h at t=0](T2), and steering had a peak of [27.0 deg at t=-0.2 s](T2), but there is no GPS lateral position or off-road flag in the data, so 'slid off the road' is unverifiable.

The vehicle experienced a crash pulse with a large longitudinal delta-V reaching a final cumulative [-75.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T2) and the EDR summary lists max longitudinal delta-V of [-76.153 km/h at 274.5 ms](T1), consistent with a significant impact with an object (single-vehicle event recorded, vehicles_in_crash [1](T1)), supporting that V1 struck a fixed object such as a utility pole.

The crash pulse is large, not 'light': cumulative longitudinal delta-V reached [ -75.6 km/h final](T2) with peak in the EDR summary of [-76.153 km/h at 274.5 ms](T1), indicating a severe impact rather than a light, glancing contact.

EDR channels show a single crash pulse recorded in the delta-V time series with a single large longitudinal accumulation reaching [ -75.6 km/h final at t=0.24 s](T2) and EDR summary lists one prior event and vehicle count [1](T1), supporting a single impact.
Whether the vehicle required only cosmetic repairs is not recorded in vehicle sensors or EDR data; repair severity cannot be inferred from the provided telemetry and is therefore unverifiable.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a single, large-magnitude crash pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V around [ -75.6 km/h](T2) and EDR max delta-V [ -76.153 km/h at 274.5 ms](T1), which supports that V1 impacted an object but contradicts the characterization of the contact as 'light, glancing'. Several narrative elements about road position and damage extent are unverifiable from the available channels.

citations.claim: 83.0 km/h at t=-4.7 s to 80.0 km/h at t=0

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
